description: Benchmark tasks for real agency-agents roles from @msitarzewski. Each role is tested with domain-specific prompts that measure how well an LLM performs when acting as that specialized agent.

roles.name: Frontend Developer
roles.description: Modern web application and UI implementation specialist. Builds responsive, accessible, performant web apps with React/Vue/Angular.

tasks.name: React Data Table Component
tasks.prompt: Build a React TypeScript component for a virtualized data table with 10,000+ rows. Requirements: sortable columns (click header to toggle asc/desc), filterable with search input, proper ARIA labels (role='table', role='row', role='cell'), keyboard navigation between rows, responsive (stacks on mobile). Use @tanstack/react-virtual. Include full component code with types.

correctness.rubric: Component renders, virtualization works, sorting/filtering functional, types correct

completeness.rubric: All 5 requirements implemented: virtualization, sort, filter, a11y, responsive

code_quality.rubric: TypeScript types, memo/useCallback, clean separation

robustness.rubric: Handles empty data, debounced filter, sort edge cases

documentation.rubric: Props interface documented, usage example

tasks.name: CSS Animation System
tasks.prompt: Create a CSS-only animation system for a card component with these states: idle (subtle float), hover (lift + shadow), active (press down), loading (shimmer skeleton). Use CSS custom properties for timing/easing configuration. Must respect prefers-reduced-motion. Include the HTML structure and complete CSS.

correctness.rubric: All 4 states animate correctly, transitions smooth, custom properties work

completeness.rubric: All states, custom properties, prefers-reduced-motion media query

code_quality.rubric: Clean CSS, no magic numbers, maintainable custom property system

robustness.rubric: Reduced motion fallback is meaningful, not just animation:none

documentation.rubric: Custom property reference table provided

tasks.name: Performance Audit Fix

correctness.rubric: Identifies: moment/lodash bundle size, unoptimized image, key={i}, sort on every render, no memoization

completeness.rubric: At least 6 issues found: bundle, image, key, sort, memo, navigation, missing lazy load

code_quality.rubric: Fixed version uses: date-fns/native sort, next/image or srcset, proper keys, useMemo, Link

robustness.rubric: Explains Lighthouse impact of each fix

documentation.rubric: Before/after bundle size or score estimate

roles.name: Backend Architect
roles.description: System architecture and server-side development specialist. Designs scalable database schemas, APIs, and cloud infrastructure.

tasks.name: Database Schema Design
tasks.prompt: Design a PostgreSQL schema for a multi-tenant SaaS project management tool. Requirements: tenant isolation via row-level security, projects with tasks (nested subtasks), team members with roles (admin/manager/member), activity audit log, file attachments with metadata. Include: CREATE TABLE statements with proper types and constraints, indexes for common queries (tasks by project, tasks by assignee, activity by date range), and the RLS policies. Optimize for a read-heavy workload with 100k+ tasks per tenant.

correctness.rubric: Valid SQL, RLS policies work, foreign keys correct, indexes useful

completeness.rubric: All entities: tenants, projects, tasks (recursive), members, roles, audit log, attachments

code_quality.rubric: Proper types (UUID, TIMESTAMPTZ, JSONB where appropriate), naming conventions consistent

robustness.rubric: Handles: subtask depth, soft deletes, concurrent access, large attachment metadata

documentation.rubric: Index justification, query pattern notes

tasks.name: API Rate Limiter Design
tasks.prompt: Design and implement a distributed rate limiter in Python that supports: sliding window algorithm, per-user and per-endpoint limits, burst allowance (token bucket), Redis-backed for distributed deployment, graceful degradation if Redis is down (fallback to local), proper HTTP headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining, Retry-After). Provide the implementation as a FastAPI middleware.

correctness.rubric: Sliding window math correct, token bucket logic right, Redis ops atomic (MULTI/EXEC or Lua)

completeness.rubric: All 6 requirements: sliding window, per-user+endpoint, burst, Redis, fallback, headers

code_quality.rubric: Clean middleware pattern, async Redis ops, proper error handling

robustness.rubric: Redis failure doesn't crash app, race conditions handled, clock skew considered

documentation.rubric: Configuration examples, rate limit strategy explanation

tasks.name: Event-Driven Architecture
tasks.prompt: Design an event-driven order processing system. Components: Order Service, Payment Service, Inventory Service, Notification Service. Requirements: eventual consistency, idempotent event handlers, dead letter queue for failed events, event schema with versioning, compensation (saga) pattern for payment failures that trigger inventory rollback. Provide: event schemas (JSON), service interaction diagram (text-based), and the saga orchestrator pseudocode.

correctness.rubric: Event flow correct, saga compensation handles all failure modes, idempotency keys present

completeness.rubric: All 4 services interact, DLQ documented, schema versioning shown, saga covers payment+inventory rollback

code_quality.rubric: Event schemas well-typed, versioning strategy clear (v1/v2 coexistence), pseudocode implementable

robustness.rubric: Handles: partial failures, duplicate events, out-of-order delivery, service timeout

documentation.rubric: Clear interaction diagram, failure scenarios enumerated

roles.name: Code Reviewer
roles.description: Code review specialist providing constructive, actionable feedback on correctness, security, maintainability, and performance.

tasks.name: Security-focused Code Review
tasks.prompt:
Review this Python endpoint for ALL issues. Use priority markers (blocker/suggestion/nit):

```python
@app.route('/api/query', methods=['POST'])
def query():
    data = request.json
    sql = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '{data['name']}' AND role = '{data['role']}'"
    result = db.execute(sql)
    token = jwt.encode({'user': data['name']}, 'secret123', algorithm='HS256')
    response = make_response(jsonify({'data': result.fetchall(), 'token': token}))
    response.headers['Access-Control-Allow-Origin'] = '*'
    return response
```

correctness.rubric: Identifies ALL: SQL injection, hardcoded JWT secret, permissive CORS, no input validation, no auth, data exposure

completeness.rubric: 6+ vulnerabilities with OWASP mapping, fix for each

code_quality.rubric: Uses parameterized queries, env secrets, specific CORS, input validation in fixes

robustness.rubric: Prioritizes by severity, provides corrected code

documentation.rubric: Uses blocker/suggestion/nit markers consistently

tasks.name: Performance Code Review

correctness.rubric: Finds: O(n^2) nested loop, full file in memory, string date comparison, sort vs heapq

completeness.rubric: 4+ issues found with fixes: streaming reads, dict lookup, datetime parse, heapq.nlargest

code_quality.rubric: Refactored version correct and idiomatic

robustness.rubric: Complexity improvement quantified

documentation.rubric: Before/after O() analysis

tasks.name: Shell Script Safety Review
tasks.prompt:
Review this deployment script for safety and correctness issues:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
MODEL=$1
docker stop $(docker ps -q)
docker rm $(docker ps -aq)
rm -rf /tmp/model-cache/*
wget -O /models/$MODEL.gguf https://huggingface.co/downloads/$MODEL
docker run -d --gpus all -v /models:/data server --model /data/$MODEL.gguf
echo "Deployed $MODEL"
```

correctness.rubric: Finds: unquoted $1, blind docker stop all, rm -rf, no checksum, no error handling, no set -euo pipefail

completeness.rubric: 7+ issues: input validation, quoting, blast radius, cleanup, wget verify, error handling, logging

code_quality.rubric: Fixed version with validation, specific container, checksum, trap, set -euo pipefail

robustness.rubric: Handles wget/docker failure, model already loaded

documentation.rubric: Severity ratings per issue

roles.name: Security Engineer
roles.description: Application security specialist. Threat modeling, OWASP analysis, secure code review, security architecture design.

tasks.name: Threat Model STRIDE Analysis
tasks.prompt:
Perform a STRIDE threat model for this architecture: A Jetson edge device runs an LLM inference API (HTTP, port 8000) on a local network. Users SSH in to manage models. A hot-swap API (port 8001) switches models based on agent role requests. Models are stored on NVMe. The device has no firewall configured. Network includes IoT devices on the same subnet.

For each STRIDE category (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege), identify at least 2 threats with severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low) and concrete mitigations.

correctness.rubric: Threats realistic and specific to the architecture (not generic), severities appropriate

completeness.rubric: All 6 STRIDE categories with 2+ threats each = 12+ total threats with mitigations

code_quality.rubric: Mitigations are specific (e.g., 'iptables rule to restrict port 8000 to VLAN X' not 'add firewall')

robustness.rubric: Considers IoT lateral movement, SSH key management, model poisoning, API abuse

documentation.rubric: Structured table or list format, actionable priority order

tasks.name: Secure API Endpoint
tasks.prompt:
This FastAPI endpoint handles model uploads for a Jetson device. Find all security issues and provide the hardened version:

```python
@app.post('/upload-model')
async def upload_model(file: UploadFile, model_name: str):
    path = f'/models/{model_name}.gguf'
    with open(path, 'wb') as f:
        content = await file.read()
        f.write(content)
    os.chmod(path, 0o777)
    return {'path': path, 'size': len(content)}
```

correctness.rubric: Finds: path traversal, no size limit (OOM), 777 permissions, no auth, no file type validation, no checksum

completeness.rubric: 6+ issues with fixes: sanitize name, size limit, 644 perms, auth middleware, magic byte check, streaming write

code_quality.rubric: Hardened version uses pathlib, streaming chunk writes, proper permissions, auth dependency

robustness.rubric: Handles partial upload cleanup, concurrent uploads, disk space check

documentation.rubric: Each vulnerability explained with attack scenario

tasks.name: Security Headers Audit

correctness.rubric: Identifies: no HTTPS, missing security headers, autoindex on, no rate limiting, server tokens visible

completeness.rubric: Adds: HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, CSP, Referrer-Policy, server_tokens off, rate_limit_zone, SSL redirect

code_quality.rubric: Complete nginx config provided, headers correct, rate limit zone properly configured

robustness.rubric: Considers local network context (HSTS may not apply, but still recommended)

documentation.rubric: Each header explained with what attack it prevents

roles.name: Technical Writer
roles.description: Developer documentation architect. README files, API references, tutorials, and technical guides.

tasks.name: Project README
tasks.prompt: Write a README.md for a Jetson LLM benchmark tool called 'jetson-bench'. Requirements: project title with one-line description, CI/coverage badge placeholders, prerequisites (JetPack 6.2+, Docker, 8GB+ RAM), install section (git clone + docker pull), usage with 3 examples showing different commands and their output, configuration table (6+ options with type/default/description), troubleshooting section with 3 common issues and solutions, contributing link, MIT license badge. Must pass the 5-second test. Under 180 lines.

correctness.rubric: All 8 sections present, valid markdown, badges formatted correctly

completeness.rubric: Every specified element included, no TODO placeholders

code_quality.rubric: Proper markdown hierarchy (h1>h2>h3), code blocks with language tags, table formatting

robustness.rubric: Commands realistic, config options sensible, troubleshooting actionable

documentation.rubric: Under 180 lines, scannable, passes 5-second test

tasks.name: API Reference Documentation

correctness.rubric: Valid OpenAPI 3.1 structure, schemas typed correctly, examples match schemas

completeness.rubric: All 3 endpoints fully documented, all error codes, rate limit headers, 9 curl examples total

code_quality.rubric: YAML well-formatted, $ref used for shared schemas, consistent naming

robustness.rubric: Error responses realistic (409 swap in progress, 503 model load fail)

documentation.rubric: Curl examples syntactically correct and runnable

tasks.name: Step-by-Step Tutorial
tasks.prompt:
Write a tutorial: 'Deploy Your First LLM on Jetson in 10 Minutes'. Target audience: developer who has a Jetson but has never run an LLM. Include:
- What they'll build (working LLM API)
- Prerequisites checklist with verification commands
- Step-by-step (6-8 steps) with exact commands
- Expected output for each step (show what success looks like)
- 'Try it out' section with example prompts
- Troubleshooting for 3 common pitfalls
- Next steps with links

correctness.rubric: Commands work on JetPack 6.2, Docker commands valid, API calls correct

completeness.rubric: All sections present, 6-8 steps, expected output shown, troubleshooting actionable

code_quality.rubric: Markdown well-structured, code blocks with language tags, copy-pasteable commands

robustness.rubric: Handles common failures: Docker not running, port in use, OOM

documentation.rubric: Beginner-friendly tone, celebrates milestones, under 15 min read

roles.name: AI Engineer
roles.description: AI/ML engineer specializing in model development, deployment, and production integration. MLOps, inference optimization, RAG systems.

tasks.name: Edge Inference Optimization
tasks.prompt: Design an inference optimization pipeline for deploying a 4B parameter LLM on a Jetson Orin Nano Super (8GB unified RAM, 1024 CUDA cores SM 8.7, 68 GB/s memory bandwidth). Cover: quantization strategy selection (GPTQ vs AWQ vs GGUF with tradeoffs), KV cache management for 8K context in limited memory, batching strategy for single-user vs multi-user, thermal management (sustained vs burst inference), and memory budget breakdown (OS + CUDA context + model weights + KV cache + compute scratch). Provide specific numbers.

correctness.rubric: Memory math accurate (OS ~1GB, CUDA ~0.5GB, model ~4GB Q8, KV ~136MB), bandwidth-limited tok/s formula correct

completeness.rubric: All 5 topics covered: quant strategy, KV management, batching, thermal, memory budget

code_quality.rubric: Specific numbers with units, formulas shown, tradeoffs quantified

robustness.rubric: Considers unified memory implications, swap pressure, GPU/CPU memory contention

documentation.rubric: Actionable recommendations with priority order

tasks.name: RAG Pipeline Architecture
tasks.prompt: Design a RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) system that runs entirely on a single Jetson device with 8GB RAM. The knowledge base is 500 technical documents (~50MB text). Cover: embedding model selection (must fit alongside LLM), vector store choice (FAISS vs ChromaDB vs SQLite-VSS with memory implications), chunking strategy, retrieval pipeline, prompt construction with retrieved context, and the complete Python implementation of the retrieval + generation pipeline.

correctness.rubric: Memory budget realistic (embedding model + vector index + LLM must fit in 8GB), retrieval pipeline works

completeness.rubric: All 6 components: embedding choice, vector store, chunking, retrieval, prompt construction, implementation

code_quality.rubric: Implementation is runnable Python, proper error handling, efficient memory use

robustness.rubric: Handles: empty results, context too long, embedding model OOM, concurrent queries

documentation.rubric: Memory budget table, performance expectations stated

tasks.name: Model Evaluation Framework
tasks.prompt: Design a benchmark framework that evaluates LLMs across multiple agent roles on edge hardware. Requirements: role-specific prompt sets with grading rubrics, automated scoring (regex + LLM-as-judge), statistical rigor (3+ runs, median + stddev), hardware metrics collection (tok/s, memory, temperature, power), result comparison across models, and exportable reports. Provide the Python class structure and the scoring pipeline.

correctness.rubric: Statistical methodology sound (median for tok/s, mean for quality), scoring pipeline separates generation from evaluation

completeness.rubric: All 6 requirements: role prompts, auto scoring, statistics, hardware metrics, comparison, reports

code_quality.rubric: Clean class hierarchy, async benchmark runner, JSON result format

robustness.rubric: Handles model crash, timeout, inconsistent output formats

documentation.rubric: Usage example, result format documented

roles.name: Performance Benchmarker
roles.description: Performance testing and optimization specialist. Load testing, bottleneck analysis, metrics-driven optimization recommendations.

tasks.name: Inference Performance Analysis
tasks.prompt:
Analyze this LLM inference performance data and provide optimization recommendations:

Device: Jetson Orin Nano Super 8GB
Model: Qwen3.5-4B Q8_0 (4.48 GB)
Runtime: llama.cpp b8095

Results (5 runs, coding prompt 512 tokens):
- Generation: 10.2, 10.5, 10.4, 10.6, 10.3 tok/s
- Prompt processing: 245, 257, 253, 261, 249 tok/s  
- RAM used: 6.1, 6.1, 6.2, 6.1, 6.1 GiB
- GPU temp: 58, 61, 63, 65, 62 °C
- Swap used: 62, 64, 66, 68, 65 MB

Theoretical bandwidth limit: 68 GB/s / 4.48 GB = 15.2 tok/s
Actual efficiency: 10.4/15.2 = 68.4%

Explain: why only 68% efficiency, what the bottleneck is, whether the thermal trend is concerning, what optimizations could close the gap, and whether swapping to NVMe indicates a problem.

correctness.rubric: Identifies memory bandwidth as primary bottleneck, kernel dispatch overhead as efficiency gap, thermal trend mild but worth monitoring

completeness.rubric: All 5 questions answered: efficiency gap, bottleneck, thermal, optimizations, swap analysis

code_quality.rubric: Statistical analysis: mean, stddev, confidence interval for key metrics

robustness.rubric: Optimizations realistic for Jetson (flash attention, mlock, context tuning) not 'upgrade hardware'

documentation.rubric: Recommendations prioritized by expected impact

tasks.name: Load Test Design
tasks.prompt: Design a load testing plan for an LLM inference API on Jetson (single GPU, one model at a time, max ~10.5 tok/s generation). The API serves agent requests from 6 different roles. Some roles need long responses (500+ tokens) and some need short structured output (50 tokens). Design: test scenarios (baseline, sustained load, burst, role-switching), key metrics to capture, success criteria, test duration, and the k6 or curl-based test script.

correctness.rubric: Scenarios realistic for single-GPU constraint (queuing, not parallel inference)

completeness.rubric: All 5 elements: scenarios, metrics, criteria, duration, script

code_quality.rubric: Test script syntactically correct and runnable

robustness.rubric: Addresses: what happens under queue saturation, timeout handling, role-switch latency

documentation.rubric: Clear pass/fail criteria with specific thresholds

tasks.name: Comparative Benchmark Report
tasks.prompt:
Write a performance comparison report for 3 LLM models on the same Jetson hardware:

| Model | Size | Gen tok/s | Prompt tok/s | RAM | Swap | Temp |
|-------|------|-----------|-------------|-----|------|------|
| Qwen3.5-4B Q8_0 | 4.48 GB | 10.5 | 257 | 6.1 GiB | 66 MB | 62°C |
| Qwen3-8B Q5_K_M | 5.5 GB | 8.2 | 180 | 6.8 GiB | 320 MB | 68°C |
| Qwen3.5-9B Q4_K_M | 5.3 GB | 8.8 | 195 | 6.6 GiB | 210 MB | 65°C |

Include: executive summary, methodology notes, per-model analysis, head-to-head comparison, efficiency analysis (tok/s per GB model weight), thermal sustainability assessment, and recommendation with reasoning.

correctness.rubric: Efficiency calculations correct, thermal analysis reasonable, comparison fair

completeness.rubric: All 7 sections present with substantive content

code_quality.rubric: Professional formatting, consistent units, clear tables

robustness.rubric: Notes swap usage as warning for 8B, thermal headroom analysis

documentation.rubric: Actionable recommendation with confidence level

roles.name: API Tester
roles.description: API testing and validation specialist. Endpoint coverage, security testing, performance validation, test automation.

tasks.name: API Test Suite Generation
tasks.prompt: Generate a complete test suite for an OpenAI-compatible LLM inference API at http://localhost:8000/v1. Endpoints: POST /v1/chat/completions, GET /v1/models, GET /v1/health. The test suite must cover: happy path (valid chat completion request), input validation (missing 'messages' field, invalid model name, empty messages array, max_tokens=0), error handling (model not loaded returns 503), performance (response time < 30s for 100 token generation), and security (no unexpected headers exposed, proper content-type). Write in Python using pytest with clear test names.

correctness.rubric: Tests target the right endpoints, assertions correct, pytest patterns proper

completeness.rubric: All categories covered: happy path, validation (4 cases), errors, performance, security = 10+ tests

code_quality.rubric: Clean pytest style, fixtures for base URL/headers, descriptive test names, no hardcoded URLs in tests

robustness.rubric: Tests independent (no order dependency), proper cleanup, timeout handling

documentation.rubric: Test categories grouped with docstrings explaining what each validates

tasks.name: Contract Testing Design
tasks.prompt:
Design a contract test that validates an LLM hot-swap API maintains backward compatibility. The API has these guaranteed behaviors:
1. POST /api/switch always returns {model, status, swap_time_ms}
2. GET /api/status always returns {model, uptime_s}
3. Response times for /api/status < 100ms
4. Model names follow pattern: [A-Za-z0-9.-]+
5. Status field only contains: 'ready', 'loading', 'error'

Write the contract tests and a CI pipeline that runs them on every PR.

correctness.rubric: Contract assertions match all 5 specifications, regex patterns correct

completeness.rubric: All 5 contracts tested, CI pipeline defined, failure handling clear

code_quality.rubric: Tests use schema validation (jsonschema or pydantic), CI YAML valid

robustness.rubric: Handles: API down during tests, slow responses, unexpected extra fields (open schema)

documentation.rubric: Contract document separate from tests, listing each guarantee

tasks.name: Failure Scenario Matrix
tasks.prompt: Create a comprehensive failure scenario test matrix for an LLM inference API on edge hardware. Categories: network failures (connection reset, timeout, DNS), resource exhaustion (OOM, disk full, GPU overtemp), application errors (invalid model, corrupt weights, context overflow), concurrent access (simultaneous requests, model swap during inference, rapid reconnection). For each scenario: describe the test setup, expected behavior, actual test implementation (curl or Python), and acceptance criteria.

correctness.rubric: Scenarios realistic for edge deployment, expected behaviors reasonable (graceful degradation)

completeness.rubric: All 4 categories with 3+ scenarios each = 12+ total test cases

code_quality.rubric: Test implementations are runnable, setup/teardown described, assertions clear

robustness.rubric: Acceptance criteria specific (e.g., 'returns 503 within 5s' not 'returns error')

documentation.rubric: Matrix format with clear risk/priority per scenario

hot_swap_config.description: Hot-swap API enables automatic model switching based on agent role. Connects to agency-agents roles via a lightweight HTTP API on the Jetson.
hot_swap_config.strategy_description: Batch requests using the same model together. Most agency-agents roles work well with the 4B default. Only code_reviewer needs the 9B model for deeper analysis.
